conversation.content:
Web search returned nothing useful. Let me proceed from local evidence. Program 3 (0.8933) is the best, combining the Hill saturating denominator with monomer-modulated plateau. The key principle: secondary-nucleation flux saturates (Meisl/Knowles), and the denominator's half-saturation point `c[6]` directly sets the burst inflection/half-response timing — the dominant shape-loss driver.

The current parent (0.8910) lacks the Hill denominator. The most disciplined improvement: graft Program 3's proven Hill-saturation + monomer-modulated plateau structure onto the current parent, but keep the bounded time onset which Program 3 also kept. Essentially I'll move the current parent toward Program 3's structure, while making one targeted refinement Program 3 didn't try: make the Hill half-saturation point **seed-dependent**, so seeded curves (which lag less) shift their inflection earlier. This is physically motivated — more seed means earlier half-response — and directly targets the eval_on
...[5239 chars total]...
lection / half-response. This gives the model a per-seed timing knob that directly attacks the seed-dependent timing component of the shape loss without changing the proven growth structure.
- **Stability:** denominator `half_sat + c² ≥ half_sat = c[6]²/(1+c[10]²·seed) > 0` (seed ≥ 0), so it's strictly positive and smooth — no singularities, no fractional powers, no logs. 11 constants stays well under the 13 limit, and replacing the free cubic with a saturating denominator improves conditioning.
